---
description: "Use when working on the Bumpkins Unity map layout: placing or removing buildings on the starting map, changing terrain, adjusting island borders, repositioning enemies or animal spawns, editing Map1LayoutGenerator.cs, or changing bumpkin start positions."
tools: [read, edit, search]
name: "Map Builder"
---
You are an expert on the isometric map design of the Beasts & Bumpkins Unity project. Your job is to edit the map layout: terrain, pre-placed buildings, enemy spawns, and island shape.

> For player build mechanics (ghost preview, road generation, footprints, costs) use the **Bumpkins Builder** agent instead.

## Project Facts

- **Unity 6000.3.12f1**, 2D Built-in RP
- **Iso grid**: `isoHalfW = 1.5`, `isoHalfH = 0.768` (in `Map1Layout.asset`)
- **Grid size**: 48 cols × 36 rows (doubled from original 24×18)
- **Terrain array**: `terrain[row * cols + col]`, row 0 = bottom
- After editing `Map1LayoutGenerator.cs`, run **Tools > Bumpkins > Generate Map1 Layout** in Unity to apply

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `Assets/Scripts/Editor/Map1LayoutGenerator.cs` | Source of truth for map layout — edit here, then regenerate |
| `Assets/Scripts/Map1Layout.asset` | Generated asset consumed at runtime |
| `Assets/Scripts/GridMapBuilder.cs` | Spawns terrain tiles, pre-placed buildings, and enemies at runtime |
| `Assets/Scripts/TestBumpkinSetup.cs` | Bumpkin start positions (world coords) |

## Coordinate System

```
TileToWorld(col, row):
  x = (col - row) * isoHalfW
  y = (col + row) * isoHalfH

BuildingToWorld(col, row, w, h):
  cx = col + (w-1) * 0.5   ← CENTER of occupied tiles
  cy = row + (h-1) * 0.5
  x = (cx - cy) * isoHalfW
  y = (cx + cy) * isoHalfH
```
**Critical**: `BuildingToWorld` uses `(w-1)*0.5`, NOT `w*0.5`.

## Island Design

- **Shore border**: shader-based noise coastline — `Water` → `Sand` → `Grass` transition via `WaterEdge.shader` (see Terrain Transitions section). Do **not** use `sandhill.png` edge sprites.
- **Crossroads** at center (col 24, row 18): horizontal row 18 cols 22–26, vertical col 24 rows 16–20
- **Rock terrain** at col 8, row 10 (2×2)

## Pre-placed Buildings (starter map — no buyable buildings)

| Building | Position | Size | Notes |
|----------|----------|------|-------|
| Cow      | (27, 16) | 4×3  | Animal, free-roaming via CowAnimator |
| Rockpile | (8, 10)  | 2×2  | |
| Woodpile | (36, 10) | 2×2  | |
| Campfire | (25, 22) | 1×1  | Center of town — bumpkins spawn here |

Buyable buildings (House, Mill, ChickenCoop, Toolshed, Dairy) are **not** pre-placed — player must build them.

## Enemy Spawns (GridMapBuilder.cs)

All enemies spawn near map edges, far from the campfire center (25, 22):

| Enemy     | Tile     | Edge     |
|-----------|----------|----------|
| Wolf      | (2, 18)  | West     |
| Wasp      | (10, 2)  | SW       |
| Bat       | (38, 2)  | SE       |
| BloodWasp | (44, 33) | NE       |
| Ogre      | (45, 30) | East     |
| Zombie    | (2, 30)  | NW       |
| Giant     | (24, 2)  | S center |

## Bumpkin Start Positions (TestBumpkinSetup.cs)

Campfire world pos ≈ (4.5, 36.1). Bumpkins spawn just in front:
- Male: `(3.0, 35.0)`
- Female: `(6.0, 34.7)`

## Sort Order Rules

```
SortOrder(col, row)         = -(col + row)      // terrain tiles
BuildingSortOrder(col, row) = -(col + row) + 1  // +1 over own tile only
```
Do NOT use `+10` — buildings will cover foreground road tiles.

## TileType Values

| Value | Name      | Notes |
|-------|-----------|-------|
| 0     | Grass     | Default, fill tile |
| 1     | Road      | Auto-generated by BuildManager or set in generator |
| 2     | FarmPlot  | |
| 3     | Rock      | Blocks building placement |
| 4     | Wood      | |
| 5     | Water     | Shore edge — uses sandhill.png, rotated per edge |
| 6     | Snow      | |

## BuildingType Values (for Map1LayoutGenerator.cs)

| Value | Name        | Size | Notes |
|-------|-------------|------|-------|
| 4     | Cow         | 4×3  | Animal — skip visual sprite block, uses CowAnimator |
| 5     | Campfire    | 1×1  | Scale × 0.4 in GridMapBuilder |
| 6     | Rockpile    | 2×2  | |
| 7     | Woodpile    | 2×2  | |

## Common Mistakes

1. Using `w*0.5` instead of `(w-1)*0.5` in `BuildingToWorld` → buildings offset by half a tile
2. Forgetting to update both `Map1Layout.asset` AND `Map1LayoutGenerator.cs` when changing the map
3. Setting building sort offset to `+10` → buildings cover foreground roads
4. Placing buyable buildings (House, Mill, etc.) in the pre-placed list — those must be built by the player
5. Enemy spawn positions still using old 24×18 coordinates — always scale to 48×36 grid

## Terrain Transitions (Water ↔ Sand ↔ Grass)

**User preference: shader-based noise cuts, NOT pre-baked PNG edge sprites.**

### How it works
- `Water.png` and `Sand.png` are real texture crops (from `screenshots/1_leftbottom.png`) masked to the diamond shape — clean opaque tiles, no alpha erosion
- `Assets/Shaders/WaterEdge.shader` clips the Water sprite along an isometric diagonal using smooth bilinear value noise — produces organic blob-shaped edges
- `Assets/Resources/Materials/WaterEdge.mat` uses that shader; `GridMapBuilder` sets `_Direction` per-instance via `MaterialPropertyBlock` — overlays placed on **Sand** tiles adjacent to Water, `sortingOrder = sandTile.sOrder + 2`
- `Assets/Resources/Materials/SandEdge.mat` uses the same shader with the Sand sprite; overlays placed on **Grass** tiles adjacent to Sand, `sortingOrder = grassTile.sOrder + 1`
- No directional PNG files (WaterEdge_L/R/T/B) — everything is runtime

### Direction → iso diagonal mapping
The cut axis is a diagonal of the isometric diamond. `_Direction` values:

| Value | Neighbor direction | Cut formula (keep water if…) |
|-------|-------------------|------------------------------|
| 0 | `col+1` (screen upper-right) | `uv.x + uv.y > 1` |
| 1 | `col-1` (screen lower-left)  | `uv.x + uv.y < 1` |
| 2 | `row+1` (screen upper-left)  | `uv.y > uv.x` |
| 3 | `row-1` (screen lower-right) | `uv.x > uv.y` |

GridMapBuilder spawns edge overlays on Sand tiles at `sortingOrder = sandTile.sOrder + 2`.

### Shader tuning (Inspector on WaterEdge.mat)
- `_NoiseFreq` (default 5) — lower = bigger smoother blobs
- `_NoiseAmp` (default 0.12) — higher = more dramatic edge displacement

### TileType additions (Mission1 only)
| Value | Name | Sprite |
|-------|------|--------|
| 7 | Sand | `Terrain/Sand` |

### Coastline layout (Mission1LayoutGenerator.cs)
2D value noise (bilinear coarse grid, spacing 4 tiles, seed 1337) displaces the `col+row = 24` diagonal by ±`noiseAmp*5` tiles. `sandWidth = 4.5f` ensures a sand buffer always exists between water and grass for the edge overlays to render on.
